back_translation: Sons you are to the Lord your God; you shall not cut yourselves and you shall not make a baldness between your eyes for the dead.
For a holy people you are to the Lord your God, and in you the Lord has chosen to be to Him a people of treasured possession from all the peoples that are on the face of the earth.
You shall not eat any abomination.
This is the animal that you may eat: ox, lamb of the flocks, and kid of the goats,
deer and gazelle and roebuck, and wild goat and antelope and wild ox and mountain sheep.
And every animal that parts the hoof and divides the cleft into two hooves, that brings up the cud among animals, you may eat it.

But this you shall not eat from those that bring up the cud and from those that part the divided hoof: the camel and the rabbit and the rock badger, for they bring up the cud and the hoof they have not parted; unclean they are to you.
And the pig, for it parts the hoof and not the cud; unclean it is to you. From their meat you shall not eat and their carcass you shall not touch.
This you may eat from all that is in the water: all that has to it fin and scale you may eat.
And all that has not to it fin and scale, you shall not eat; unclean it is to you.
Every clean bird you may eat.
And this is what you shall not eat of them: the eagle and the bearded vulture and the black vulture,
and the red kite and the falcon and the kite according to its kind,
and every raven according to its kind,
and the ostrich and the screech owl and the gull and the hawk according to its kind,
the little owl and the great owl and the white owl,
and the desert owl and the osprey and the cormorant,
and the stork and the heron according to its kind, and the hoopoe and the bat.
And every flying swarming thing, unclean it is to you; they shall not be eaten.
Every clean bird you may eat.

You shall not eat any carcass; to the foreigner who is in your gates you may give it, and he will eat it, or sell to a stranger, for a holy people you are to the Lord your God. You shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk.
Tithing you shall tithe all the produce of your seed that goes out of the field year by year.

And you shall eat before the Lord your God, in the place that He will choose to cause His name to dwell there, the tithe of your grain, your new wine, and your oil, and the firstborn of your herd and your flock, so that you may learn to fear the Lord your God all the days.

And if the way is too long for you, because you are not able to carry it, because the place is far from you that the Lord your God will choose to put His name there, when the Lord your God blesses you,
then you shall give it in money, and you shall bind the money in your hand, and you shall go to the place that the Lord your God will choose in it.

And you shall give the money for all that your soul desires, for cattle and for flock and for wine and for strong drink, and for all that your soul asks of you, and you shall eat there before the Lord your God and rejoice, you and your household.
And the Levite who is in your gates, you shall not forsake him, for he has no portion and inheritance with you.
At the end of three years, you shall bring out all the tithe of your produce in that year, and you shall lay it up in your gates.

And the Levite shall come, for he has no portion and inheritance with you, and the foreigner and the fatherless and the widow who are in your gates, and they shall eat and be satisfied, so that the Lord your God may bless you in all the work of your hand that you do.
